Q4 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Which of the following statements is/are correct?
1. If a channel section is used as a simply supported beam with its web horizontal to carry vertical loads then it will be strongest if web is used as top flange.
2. Torsional restraint in a beam can be achieved by providing external support to the ends of compression flange.
3. Web crippling is caused by excessive bending moment.

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
• Web crippling is actually local buckling that occurs when web is slender. It occurs due to high
magnitude of point load which results in stress concentrations and bearing failures.
• Torsional restraint prevents rotation at the beam ends about its end longitudinal axis.

Not attempted

A simply supported beam of ISLB 200 @ 192.2 N/m has 4 m effective span and is laterally supported throughout. It carries a load of 40 kN/m including its self weight. The design shear capacity of the beam is ______ .
Relevant properties of the section ISLB 200 are:
Width of flange, bf = 100 mm
Thickness of flange, tf = 7.4 mm
Thickness of web, tw = 5.5 mm

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)

Q8 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

The effective width of outstand in compound steel column for design purposes is equal to:

Correct answer: D
Solution

(d)
As per IS-800 : 2007, the effective width of outstand in compound steel column for design purposes
is equal to the distance of the free edge to the nearest row of rivets.

Q9 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Choose correct statements regarding rules for the design of battened columns as per IS : 800-2007:
1. Batten plates should be provided symmetrically.
2. Battens shall be of plates, angles, I sections and their ends shall be riveted, bolted or welded,
3. Transverse shear force equal to 3.5% of the total axial force is used to design batters.
4. Distance between the members of columns is so maintained in battens, so that radius of gyration about the axis perpendicular to be plane of battens is not less than the radius of gyration about the axis parallel to the plane of battens (ryy > rzz)

Correct answer: B
Solution

(b)
Statement 3: Battens shall be designed to carry the bending moments and shear force arising from
the transverse shear force equal to 2.5% of the total axial force.

Q10 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Choose the incorrect statements:
1. Vertical intermediate stiffener should be provided throughout the length of beam at spacing less than 1.5 times the depth.
2. Bearing stiffeners must be provided at the point of maximum bending moment.
3. Horizontal stiffeners are provided in the tension zone of the web.
4. In rolled steel section, dimensions of the section are so adjusted that they do not fail in buckling and serviceability criteria.

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
Bearing stiffeners are provided where buckling occurs mainly in web area.
Horizontal stiffeners are provided in the compression zone of the web.

Q11 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Bearing stiffeners are designed for bearing forces and they must also be checked for safety against:

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
Bearing stiffeners are designed for bearing force and they must be checked for compressive force.

Q12 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Choose correct statements regarding tension field method:
1. It is used to find shear buckling strength of web as stiffeners are provided.
2. It accounts for post buckling strength provided by stiffeners.
3. The web resists only diagonal tension.
4. Web loses its ability to resist diagonal tension when buckling begins.

Correct answer: B
Solution

(b)
Statements 1, 2 and 3 are correct
As the web begins to buckle, it loses it ability to resist diagonal compression.

Q13 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Horizontal stiffeners are provided when:
1. The depth of the web is small
2. Tendency of web buckling is less.
3. Vertical stiffeners become too close.
4. Only thin plates are available for web.
Which as the following statement(s) is/are correct?

Correct answer: B
Solution

(b)
Horizontal stiffener is provided when the depth of web is more and there is a tendency of web buckling.
It is provided when vertical stiffeners become too close and only thin plates are available for web.

According to IS 800 : 2007 which of the following statements are correct?
1. It recommends use of either limit state method or working stress method as per the designer’s choice.
2. Repairable damage is included under limit state of serviceability.
3. Actions are classified in two categories. They are permanent action (self weight) and variable action (impact of vehicle).

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
Refer IS 800: 2007
Cl. 5.1.4, 5.2.2.2(c) and 5.3.1

Q19 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Which of the following loads are not considered while designing the gantry girder?
1. Vertical loads
2. Longitudinal load
3. Lateral loads
4. Impact loads

Correct answer: D
Solution

(d)
Gantry girder – typically used to support cranes – must be designed to handle:
• Vertical loads: Due to self-weight of the girder, crane and lifted load.
• Longitudinal loads: Due to acceleration/deceleration of the crane in the direction of the track (braking forces etc.)
• Lateral loads: Due to the movement of the crab (cross travel) and sometimes due to skewing.
• Impact loads: Dynamic effect when the load is lifted or suddenly applied.

Q20 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

The design wind speed is assumed to be constant from the mean ground level up to a height of

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
According to IS 875 (Part 3): Wind Loads, the design wind speed is generally considered constant up to a height of 10 meters above ground level.
Above this height, wind speed increases with height due to reduced surface friction and is adjusted using terrain and topography factors.

Q21 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Which of the following is not true with regard to the assumptions made in the plastic analysis?

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
Symmetry of cross-section is not an assumption in plastic analysis.
While symmetrical sections are commonly used, plastic analysis can be applied to unsymmetrical sections as well.

3. It is the ratio of elastic section modulus to plastic section modulus of the section.
4. It gives an idea of the margin provided by the reserve of strength.

Q34 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

In laced columns, end tie plates are provided to

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
Tie plates are not meant to resist overall column buckling or individual member buckling; they are
specifically provided to prevent distortion at the ends due to unbalanced forces from the lacing
system.

Q37 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

The following items consists of two statements, labelled as Statement I and Statement II. You have to examine the two statements and select your answer using the codes given below:
Codes:
(a) Both Statement I and Statement II are correct and Statement II is the correct explanation of Statement I.
(b) Both Statement I and Statement II are correct but Statement II is not the correct explanation of Statement I.
(c) Statement I is true but Statement II is false.
(d) Statement I is false but Statement II is true.

Statement I: The strength of weld is affected by the presence of slag within the weld.
Statement II: Slag makes the connection with metal non-homogeneous.

Correct answer: A
Solution

(a)
Strength of weld is affected due to non-homogeneous metal connection due to presence of slag
(oxides of metals) in weld.

Q38 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

The following items consists of two statements, labelled as Statement I and Statement II. You have to examine the two statements and select your answer using the codes given below:
Codes:
(a) Both Statement I and Statement II are correct and Statement II is the correct explanation of Statement I.
(b) Both Statement I and Statement II are correct but Statement II is not the correct explanation of Statement I.
(c) Statement I is true but Statement II is false.
(d) Statement I is false but Statement II is true.

Statement I: From design point of view, slenderness ratio of tension member has to be within certain limits.
Statement II: Slenderness ratio of tension member ensures strength of the member within its capacity.

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
Slenderness ratio in tension member has to be in certain limits to avoid:
(i) Excessive sag due to self weight of member.
(ii) Flutter due to wind.
(iii) Vibrations caused by moving loads.
